settings.guest_visible_help: Allow guests to view magnet links. If disabled, guests will see a message asking them to register or login.
settings.activated_only_help: Only users with confirmed email can view magnet links. This setting applies to logged-in users only.
settings.scraper_enabled_help: When enabled, the extension will query trackers to get seed/leech/download statistics. When disabled, only the torrent name and click count will be displayed.
settings.scraper_engine_help: Hardened (default) = cURL engine that pins the resolved IP (closing the DNS-rebinding / SSRF window) and adds IPv6 support for UDP trackers; it requires the cURL PHP extension and verifies TLS strictly, so trackers with broken/self-signed certificates may stop responding. Classic = the original file_get_contents engine (no cURL needed) with a documented residual rebinding window — pick it only if cURL is unavailable.
settings.http_only_help: Only query HTTP and HTTPS trackers. Enable this if your hosting provider doesn't allow UDP connections.
settings.allow_private_trackers_help: By default, trackers pointing at private, loopback or reserved IP addresses are blocked to prevent the server from being used to reach internal services (SSRF). Only enable this if you intentionally run a tracker on a private network.
settings.check_all_help: Query all available trackers and aggregate results based on the display type setting.
settings.display_type_help: How to aggregate statistics when checking multiple trackers.
settings.display_type_average_max: Average for seeds/leeches, max for downloads
settings.tracker_timeout_help: Maximum time to wait for a tracker response. Set to 0 for default (2 seconds).
settings.max_trackers_help: Maximum number of trackers to contact per magnet. Set to 0 to use the built-in safety cap (12). A hard time budget also applies so a single magnet can't hang a request.
settings.topic_scrape_budget_help: Maximum total time spent scraping trackers for a magnet shown in the open topic (the in-post card). Default 15. This is the accurate, full view, so it gets a longer budget than the list tooltip. Hard-capped at 30 seconds so one request can't tie up a worker for long.
settings.scraper_max_redirects_help: How many HTTP(S) redirects to follow when scraping a tracker. Default 0 (don't follow). Raise to 1-2 if a tracker is behind Cloudflare or a CDN that redirects http→https. Every redirect hop is still validated by the SSRF guard, so a tracker can't redirect the request to a private/internal address.
settings.priority_trackers_help: Optional. One tracker host per line (e.g. tracker.opentrackr.org). If a magnet contains any of these, they are queried first, in this order — most useful in the default 'stop at first responder' mode so stats come from a tracker you trust, and it improves latency/accuracy. Reorder only: trackers that aren't in the magnet are never added, and the SSRF guard, scheme/HTTP-only filters and limits still apply.
settings.scraper_host_allowlist_help: Optional. One trusted tracker host per line. When non-empty, ONLY these hosts are ever contacted — tracker URLs supplied in post content that aren't listed are skipped entirely, closing the SSRF / DNS-rebinding surface for attacker-supplied trackers. Leave empty (default) to scrape any public host (current behaviour). The SSRF guard, scheme / HTTP-only filters and limits still apply on top.
settings.cache_enabled_help: Cache seed/leech/download stats so repeated views and tooltip hovers don't re-query the trackers. Strongly recommended — it is also the main protection against hover-driven load. Controls both the server cache and the in-browser cache.
settings.cache_ttl_help: How long cached tracker stats stay valid. Default 300 (5 minutes). Set to 0 to disable caching (not recommended).
settings.refresh_cooldown_help: Minimum time between manual refreshes of the same magnet (global — one refresh updates the shared result everyone sees, so it can't be re-refreshed sooner). 0 = no cooldown. Requires caching to be enabled to share the refreshed result.
settings.refresh_limit_count_help: Maximum number of manual refreshes a single IP (works for guests too) may perform within the window below. 0 = unlimited.
settings.refresh_limit_window_help: The rolling time window for the per-IP refresh limit. Each used refresh frees up again once it is older than this. Default 600 (10 minutes).
settings.click_tracking_help: Track and display the number of times each magnet link has been clicked.
settings.tooltip_enabled_help: Show magnet link stats when hovering over discussions in the discussion list.
settings.tooltip_max_magnets_help: Maximum number of magnet links displayed in the discussion tooltip. Set to 0 for unlimited.
settings.tooltip_scrape_budget_help: Total time budget shared across all magnets scraped for one discussion-list hover. Kept short (default 4) because the scrape runs synchronously and holds a PHP worker while you hover; the open-topic card uses the larger topic budget for full accuracy. Raise it if your magnets have many trackers and the tooltip often shows 'no response'. Hard-capped at 30 seconds.
settings.tooltip_max_concurrent_help: How many hover-tooltip scrape requests may run at once (default 2). When you hover past this many discussions, the newest hover aborts the oldest in-flight request. Leaving a discussion or opening one also aborts its request. This caps how many PHP workers rapid hovering over the list can tie up. Lower it (e.g. 1) on small/shared hosting; raise it (up to 6) only if your server has spare workers.
settings.tooltip_max_trackers_help: Maximum number of trackers the tooltip contacts per magnet. Set to 0 (default) to inherit the global 'Maximum Trackers to Query'. The tooltip already falls through trackers that don't respond — it's bounded by the tooltip time budget above, not by where the first dead tracker is. Set a separate value here only to give the tooltip a different cap from the in-topic card; a different value gets its own cache entry so it never poisons the topic's full result.
settings.tooltip_tracker_timeout_help: Per-tracker response timeout used only for tooltip scrapes. Set to 0 (default) to inherit the global 'Tracker Timeout'. Lowering it (e.g. 1) makes dead/slow trackers fail faster, so more trackers can be tried within the tooltip time budget — the most effective way to make the tooltip reach a live tracker when private/dead trackers are listed first. Does not affect the in-topic card.
settings.tooltip_show_permission_errors_help: When a guest or a user without permission hovers a discussion that has magnet links, show a clear message in the tooltip ("You must be logged in…") instead of letting it briefly flash the loading state and disappear. Mirrors the in-post behaviour. Enabled by default.
settings.rename_enabled_help: Allow post authors to rename magnet link display names within their own posts.
settings.desktop_style_help: Choose how the in-post magnet card is laid out on desktop. Phones always use the mobile layout regardless of this setting.
settings.desktop_style_desc_standard: Standard layout: the card is a single row — icon, torrent name on one line (truncated with an ellipsis, full name in the hover tooltip), click count and the copy/refresh buttons.
settings.desktop_style_desc_mobile: Mobile layout everywhere: row 1 holds the icon plus the click count and copy/refresh buttons; the full torrent name wraps onto its own row below (so longer names stay readable); the seed/leech/download stats sit on a single line with a font size that scales to fit. Tune the wrapped name and the stats alignment below.
settings.name_max_lines_help: Maximum number of lines the wrapped torrent name may use before it is cut off with an ellipsis on the last line. Applies to the mobile layout (phones, and desktop when the Mobile style is selected).
settings.stats_justify_help: How the seed / leech / download stats are distributed across their single line in the mobile layout.
settings.ban_enabled_help: Temporarily ban IPs that click too many magnet links in a short time to prevent click count manipulation.
settings.ban_time_help: How long an IP is banned after exceeding the click limit.
settings.ban_interval_help: Time window for counting clicks to determine if an IP should be banned.
settings.ban_interval_count_help: Number of clicks within the interval that triggers a ban.
settings.self_interval_help: How long before the same IP can increment the click count for the same magnet link again.

reparse.label: Magnet backfill
reparse.help: Magnet links posted before this extension was enabled are stored as plain text, so they don't render in posts or show in tooltips. Run this once (after enabling the extension or importing old content) to re-parse them. For very large forums use the CLI instead: php flarum magnet:reparse
reparse.error: Failed to re-parse magnet links. Check the server logs.

retokenize.label: Token security
retokenize.help: Magnet tokens keep the link out of the page source so it can only be fetched through the permission-checked API. Older versions derived tokens from a public, constant salt, which let known torrents be reversed offline straight from the HTML. Run this once after upgrading to re-secure every token with a per-site secret. For very large forums use the CLI instead: php flarum magnet:retokenize
retokenize.error: Failed to re-secure tokens. Check the server logs.

maintenance.label: Table retention (large forums)
maintenance.help: magnet_clicks and magnet_bans can grow large on busy or bot-targeted forums. Pruning is opt-in by design (the topic click sorts read magnet_clicks directly, so the retention window is your call). Wire these into the server's cron, e.g. daily: php flarum magnet:prune-clicks --days=90  and  php flarum magnet:prune-bans

support.title: Support This Extension
support.description: If you find this extension useful, please consider supporting its development with a small donation. Every contribution helps keep the project alive and maintained.

forum.guest_not_allowed: You must be logged in to view magnet links.
forum.email_not_confirmed: Please confirm your email to view magnet links.
forum.permission_denied: You do not have permission to view magnet links.

editor.invalid: Invalid magnet link. The link must start with 'magnet:'

tooltip.no_magnets: No magnet links in this discussion

refresh_limit.cooldown: This magnet was just refreshed — you can refresh it again in {seconds}s. Everyone already sees the latest result.
refresh_limit.quota: Refresh limit reached. Please try again in {seconds}s.
